title: SSH Brute-Force Campaign
briefing: Overnight, monitoring flagged a spike of failed SSH logins on an internet-facing host. Two external sources appear to be hammering credentials. Triage the alerts in the Wazuh dashboard, identify the attackers and the targeted host, and recommend containment.
dashboard_hint: Discover -> filter rule.groups: authentication_failed, or rule.id: 5710. Group by data.srcip and agent.name. Look at the last 30 minutes.

questions.prompt: Which source IP(s) are conducting the attack? (comma-separated)
questions.explain: Both external IPs generated repeated rule 5710/5716 auth failures against the same host.

questions.prompt: What class of attack is this?
questions.explain: Repeated failed authentication from a single source = credential brute force / password guessing.
All the auth-failure alerts carry agent.name = web01.

questions.prompt: Which MITRE ATT&CK technique ID best fits? (e.g. T1234)

questions.explain: Sustained external brute force against an internet-facing SSH service is high severity until confirmed unsuccessful.

questions.prompt: Recommended immediate containment (select all that apply):
options: Block the source IPs at the firewall
answer: Block the source IPs at the firewall
questions.explain: Block sources, add rate-limiting/lockout, and move to key-only auth. Rebooting does nothing; ignoring is wrong.
